items.subtopic: Management of Youth-Onset Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus (T2DM)
items.question: Why are currently approved medications for youth-onset T2DM limited?
items.answer: Because the management options for youth-onset T2DM are limited due to the complexity and severity of the disease, as well as its rapid progression.
keyPoints: Youth-onset T2DM is characterized by progressive β-cell injury and insulin resistance
The condition carries higher risk and earlier onset of severe complications such as heart disease, diabetic kidney disease, and fatty liver disease
items.reference: MAIN CONCLUSIONS: "Youth-onset T2DM is a complex metabolic disorder characterized by progressive β-cell injury and insulin resistance. With the rapidly rising number of obese adolescents with T2DM, it is critical to check pancreatic autoantibodies in the workup of youth-onset T2DM. Compared with T1DM, youth-onset T2DM also carries higher risk and earlier onset of severe complications, such as heart disease, diabetic kidney disease, and fatty liver disease. Options for management are limited and have been largely understudied in youth-onset T2DM. Currently FDA-approved medications include insulin, metformin, and liraglutide."

items.question: How should pancreatic autoantibodies be checked in the workup of youth-onset T2DM?
items.answer: Pancreatic autoantibodies, such as insulin antibodies and glutamic acid decarboxylase (GAD) antibodies, can be checked to help diagnose youth-onset type 2 diabetes mellitus (T2DM). These autoantibodies are more commonly found in youth-onset T2DM compared to type 1 diabetes mellitus (T1DM), which aids in the differential diagnosis between these two conditions.
keyPoints: Pancreatic autoantibodies can be used as a diagnostic tool for youth-onset T2DM.
Their presence is more indicative of youth-onset T2DM compared to T1DM.
items.reference: MAIN CONCLUSIONS: "Youth-onset T2DM is a complex metabolic disorder characterized by progressive β-cell injury and insulin resistance. With the rapidly rising number of obese adolescents with T2DM, it is critical to check pancreatic autoantibodies in the workup of youth-onset T2DM."
